title: 怪屋
summary:
Aristide Leonidas leaves a final will, which he secretly replaces with a different version during a signing ceremony witnessed by two servants to quell suspicion. In the document, he expresses gratitude for the family's loyalty and asks Sophia to protect them. Following his death, Mr. Gasteyer of the law firm Carlum and Gasteyer & Co. expresses offense at Leonidas's perceived lack of trust, leading to a confrontation with the narrator's father. Inspector Taverna notes that Leonidas was a cunning man who avoided using Gasteyer's firm for suspicious business.

The narrator, Charles, receives an urgent call from his girlfriend, who reports that Josephine has been severely injured by a blow to the head and may not recover. Charles, his father, and Taverna travel to Swinley, where Sophia informs them that Josephine has been taken to General de Macte Barcin Hospital.

Upon arriving at the property, Sophia leads them to a dilapidated courtyard containing a laundry room. A marble lion doorstop, originally from the front door, had been placed on top of the laundry room door as a trap. Sophia explains that Josephine had a habit of swaying on the door's bottom groove. Taverna observes that the trap was likely set to fall, though it was imprecise. He notes that the killer was careful, but Charles discovers dirt fragments on an old wooden chair, suggesting someone with muddy shoes stood on it. Sophia reports finding Josephine unconscious with blood on her hair around 1:15 PM. Taverna concludes the doorstop must have been placed between breakfast and 1:00 PM.
